trade.summary: Germany is Poland's largest trading partner and Poland is Germany's fifth-largest. Trade is deeply integrated through EU single market and supply chains. Poland exports machinery, vehicles, food products, and furniture; Germany exports machinery, vehicles, chemicals, and electrical equipment. Polish manufacturing is deeply embedded in German automotive supply chains.

diplomatic.status: Important EU and NATO partners with historical complexities
diplomatic.summary: Germany and Poland have a complex relationship shaped by WWII history and modern EU politics. Under the Tusk government (since 2023), relations improved significantly after the difficult PiS era. Both cooperate closely on Ukraine support. Historical WWII reparation demands remain a sensitive background issue. Both are critical NATO partners on the eastern flank.

military.summary: Both are NATO members. Germany leads the NATO Enhanced Forward Presence battlegroup in Lithuania; Poland hosts a US-led battlegroup. Joint NATO exercises are frequent. Poland is dramatically increasing defense spending (4%+ GDP) and has become one of NATO's most capable eastern flank members. Germany-Poland military cooperation has deepened significantly since 2022.

financial.summary: Germany is Poland's largest FDI source. German firms are deeply embedded in Poland's manufacturing economy (automotive, chemicals, retail, logistics). Polish zloty-euro currency connection creates some financial interdependency.

energy.summary: Poland is transitioning away from coal and developing LNG infrastructure. Germany and Poland coordinate on Baltic Sea energy infrastructure, including Baltic Pipe (which bypasses Russia). Both are co-developing offshore wind in the Baltic Sea. Energy cooperation is growing.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific collaboration through EU Horizon programs. German and Polish universities have extensive exchange programs. Joint research in engineering, automotive, and environmental sciences. Large Polish student community in Germany.
